name: Pumpkin and Praline Pie
recipeInstructions:
- Hot Water Dough:
1. Beat lard or shortening and salt in a large bowl.
2. Gradually beat in boiling water.
3. Cool to room temperature, stirring occasionally to prevent separation of water and lard.
4. Beat in 2 cups flour; form dough into 2 balls.
5. Flatten each portion into a 6-inch disk.
6. Wrap in plastic wrap and chill (refrigerate overnight or freeze up to 1 month).
- Pumpkin Filling:
1. Mix sugar, light brown sugar, flour, bitters (optional), cinnamon, ginger, salt, nutmeg, cloves in a large bowl.
2. Stir in egg; set aside.
3. Melt butter in a large skillet over low heat.
4. Add pumpkin puree; simmer, stirring occasionally, until thickens slightly (about 10 minutes).
5. Gradually stir hot pumpkin puree into the sugar mixture.
6. Stir in evaporated milk, sweet milk, and 1 cup water.
7. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
- Praline:
1. Beat butter and brown sugar in a medium bowl.
2. Stir in pecans; set aside (cover and refrigerate overnight).
- Assemble and Bake:
1. Bring chilled dough to room temperature.
2. Working with one disk at a time, place on a floured surface.
3. Roll into a 13-inch circle, 1/8-inch thick using half of the remaining flour.
4. Transfer and fit dough into a 9-inch deep dish pie pan; trim away excess dough and flute edges with fork.
5. Repeat with remaining disk to make second pie shell.
6. Prick each pie shell all over its surface with a fork.
7. Preheat oven to 450 degrees F (232 degrees C).
8. Freeze pie shells for 10 minutes.
9. Spread half of the praline mixture over bottom of each pie shell.
10. Bake until praline is golden brown and bubbly, about 10 minutes; cool slightly.
11. Reduce oven temperature to 400 degrees F (204 degrees C).
12. Pour half the pumpkin filling into each crust; smooth top with spatula.
13. Bake until pumpkin filling is firm and crusts are golden brown, about 1 hour.
14. Cool completely and serve. Makes 2 pies. Top with whipped cream or whipped topping if desired. Garnish with whole pecans.
